questions.question_text: Does Ohio law set a maximum amount for residential security deposits?
questions.correct_answer: No, there is no statutory maximum
distractors: Yes, two months' rent maximum
Yes, one month's rent maximum
Yes, $2,000 maximum regardless of rent
questions.explanation: Ohio has no statutory maximum for security deposits. However, deposits exceeding certain thresholds trigger interest payment requirements for the landlord.

questions.question_text: A tenant pays a $1,200 security deposit and stays for 8 months. Monthly rent is $900. Must the landlord pay interest on the deposit?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, the deposit and stay both exceed thresholds
distractors: No, interest is only required after 12 months
No, interest is never required in Ohio
Only if the tenant requests it in writing
questions.explanation: Ohio requires 5% annual interest when the deposit exceeds $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) AND the tenant stays more than 6 months. Both conditions are met here.

questions.question_text: A tenant moves out of an Ohio rental. Within how many days must the landlord return the security deposit?
distractors: 14 days
45 days
60 days
questions.explanation: Ohio landlords must return the security deposit within 30 days of move-out, along with an itemized list of any deductions for damages or unpaid rent.

questions.question_text: A landlord wants to deduct unpaid rent from a tenant's security deposit. Under 2024 Ohio law, what is required?
questions.correct_answer: The tenant's consent or a court order
distractors: No special permission is needed for rent deductions
Only a written 10-day notice to the tenant
Approval from the local housing authority
questions.explanation: Ohio law provides that landlords cannot deduct unpaid rent from security deposits without the tenant's consent. If the tenant does not consent, the landlord must go through the courts.

questions.question_text: Under a 2024 Ohio update, tenants now have the option to pay security deposits in which manner?
distractors: By credit card with a 3% surcharge
Through a state-run escrow program
Via payroll deduction from their employer
questions.explanation: A 2024 update gives Ohio tenants the option to pay security deposits in installments over 3 or 6 months instead of as a lump sum upfront.

questions.question_text: Under Ohio's landlord-tenant law, which obligation does the landlord owe to tenants regarding the rental premises?
questions.correct_answer: Maintain the premises in habitable condition
distractors: Provide furnished living spaces
Pay all utility bills for the tenant
Allow pets in all rental units
questions.explanation: Ohio landlords must maintain premises in a fit and habitable condition, comply with building/health/safety codes, and provide running water, reasonable heat, and hot water.

questions.question_text: An Ohio landlord fails to repair a broken heating system after receiving written notice from the tenant. What remedy is available to the tenant?
questions.correct_answer: Deposit rent with the clerk of courts (rent escrow)
distractors: Withhold rent and keep it personally
Deduct repair costs from next month's rent
File a criminal complaint with the police
questions.explanation: Ohio tenants can deposit rent with the clerk of courts (rent escrow) when a landlord fails to maintain habitability after written notice. The tenant may also terminate the rental agreement.

questions.question_text: A tenant reports a code violation to the health department. The landlord begins eviction proceedings 10 days later. This is most likely:
distractors: Legitimate eviction for lease violation
Permissible since the tenant is month-to-month
Allowed if 3 days written notice was provided
questions.explanation: Ohio prohibits retaliatory eviction within 30 days of a tenant's complaint or exercise of legal rights. An eviction following a code violation report is presumed retaliatory.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a required landlord obligation under Ohio law?
questions.correct_answer: Keeping common areas clean and safe
distractors: Providing off-street parking for all tenants
Paying for renter's insurance for each unit
Replacing all appliances every 5 years
questions.explanation: Ohio landlords must keep common areas clean and safe. Other obligations include maintaining plumbing, heating, electrical systems, and providing running water and reasonable heat.

questions.question_text: An Ohio tenant's apartment has no hot water for two weeks despite written complaints. Besides rent escrow, what other option does the tenant have?
distractors: Sue the landlord for pain and suffering
Move out without giving notice
Contact the Governor's office directly
questions.explanation: When a landlord fails to maintain habitability after written notice, Ohio tenants may either deposit rent with the clerk of courts or terminate the rental agreement entirely.

questions.question_text: An Ohio tenant fails to pay rent. How many days' notice must the landlord give before filing for eviction?
distractors: 7 days
14 days
30 days
questions.explanation: Ohio uses a 3-day notice to vacate for nonpayment of rent. After the notice period expires, the landlord may file a forcible entry and detainer action in court.

questions.question_text: What type of court action must an Ohio landlord file to legally evict a tenant?
distractors: Small claims court complaint
Unlawful detainer petition only
Administrative hearing request
questions.explanation: Formal eviction in Ohio requires filing a forcible entry and detainer action in court. The landlord cannot evict without a court order.

questions.question_text: An Ohio landlord changes the locks on a tenant's apartment while the tenant is at work, without a court order. This is:
distractors: Permitted with 24 hours verbal notice
Legal if the tenant is behind on rent
Acceptable for month-to-month tenancies
questions.explanation: Self-help evictions — changing locks, shutting off utilities, removing tenant's belongings — are illegal in Ohio. All evictions must go through the court process.

questions.question_text: A landlord shuts off the electricity to a tenant's unit to force them to leave. Under Ohio law, this is:
distractors: Permitted if the tenant owes utility payments
Legal after providing 48 hours written notice
Acceptable if the landlord pays the utility company
questions.explanation: Shutting off utilities to force a tenant to vacate is an illegal self-help eviction in Ohio. The landlord must follow the formal court eviction process.

questions.question_text: After serving a 3-day notice, the tenant does not vacate or pay rent. What is the landlord's next step?
questions.correct_answer: File a court action for forcible entry and detainer
distractors: Change the locks and remove belongings
Contact the county sheriff to remove the tenant
Post a 30-day eviction notice on the door
questions.explanation: If the tenant does not comply with the 3-day notice, the landlord must file a forcible entry and detainer action in court. Self-help measures are not permitted.

questions.question_text: In Ohio, managing rental property for others for compensation requires what?
distractors: Only a business license from the county
A property management certificate only
No license if managing fewer than 5 units
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a real estate license to manage property for others for compensation. The property manager must operate under a licensed broker.

questions.question_text: An Ohio property manager signs a management agreement with a property owner. This agreement must be in what form?
questions.correct_answer: Written form specifying duties and compensation
distractors: Verbal form is acceptable for residential
Any form the parties agree to use
Filed with the Division within 30 days
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a written property management agreement that specifies duties, compensation, authority, and term. Verbal agreements are not sufficient for PM relationships.

questions.question_text: A property manager in Ohio collects rent from tenants. Where must these funds be deposited?
questions.correct_answer: A separate property management trust account
distractors: The broker's general operating account
The property owner's personal account directly
The same trust account used for sales escrow
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a separate trust account for property management activities. Rent, security deposits, and owner funds must be kept in this dedicated PM trust account.

questions.question_text: A tenant signs a 4-year residential lease in Ohio. For this lease to be effective against third parties (such as a future buyer), it must be:
distractors: Notarized by a licensed notary public
Filed with the Division of Real Estate
Approved by the local zoning board
questions.explanation: Leases for more than 3 years must be recorded to be effective against third parties. Recording provides constructive notice of the tenant's interest in the property.

questions.question_text: A licensed property manager must operate under whose supervision in Ohio?
distractors: The Division of Real Estate directly
Any licensed salesperson in good standing
The property owner who hired them
questions.explanation: Property managers with a real estate license must operate under a licensed broker, just like salespersons involved in sales transactions. The broker supervises PM activities.
